content:
# Luke Chapter 20 — Translation Disagreements

**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Translators:** EMET (Literal), LOGOS (Formal), PNEUMA (Dynamic)
**Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), PDF pages 113-114

---

## Verse-by-Verse Disagreements

### 20:1 — ΕΠΕΣΤΗΣΑΝ ("came upon")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "stood-upon [him]" | Hyper-literal with brackets |
| LOGOS | "came upon him" | Formal, captures confrontational force |
| PNEUMA | "came up to him" | Loses aggressive nuance |
| **SOPHIA** | **"came upon him"** | **Follows LOGOS — confrontational without brackets** |

### 20:1 — ΕΥΑΓΓΕΛΙΖΟΜΕΝΟΥ ("proclaiming the good news")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "proclaiming-the-good-news" | Hyphenated compound |
| LOGOS | "proclaiming the gospel" | Imports technical term "gospel" |
| PNEUMA | "proclaiming the good news" | Transparent rendering |
| **SOPHIA** | **"proclaiming the good news"** | **Follows PNEUMA — transparent without hyphens** |

### 20:6 — ΠΕΠΕΙΣΜΕΝΟΣ ΕΣΤΙΝ ... ΠΡΟΦΗΤΗΝ ("persuaded ... prophet")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "are having been persuaded [that] John is a prophet" | Over-literal; retains present tense "is" |
| LOGOS | "are persuaded that John was a prophet" | Past tense "was" in indirect discourse |
| PNEUMA | "are convinced that John was a prophet" | Past tense "was" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"are persuaded that John was a prophet"** | **Follows LOGOS — "was" appropriate since John has been executed** |

### 20:9 — ΓΕΩΡΓΟΙΣ ("tenants")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "tenant-farmers" | Hyphenated compound |
| LOGOS | "tenants" | Concise |
| PNEUMA | "tenant farmers" | Two words, no hyphen |
| **SOPHIA** | **"tenants"** | **Follows LOGOS — concise; vineyard context is self-evident** |

### 20:17 — ΚΕΦΑΛΗΝ ΓΩΝΙΑΣ ("head of the corner")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "head of [the] corner" | Brackets for article |
| LOGOS | "the head of the corner" | Full formal rendering |
| PNEUMA | "the cornerstone" | Single-word interpretation |
| **SOPHIA** | **"the head of the corner"** | **Follows LOGOS — preserves the Greek metaphor faithfully** |

### 20:18 — ΛΙΚΜΗΣΕΙ ΑΥΤΟΝ ("will crush him")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "it will crush him" | Concise |
| LOGOS | "it will scatter him like dust" | Adds "like dust" interpretively |
| PNEUMA | "it will crush anyone on whom it falls" | Restructures clause |
| **SOPHIA** | **"it will crush him"** | **Follows EMET — concise; LOGOS adds words not in the Greek** |

### 20:21 — ΟΥ ΛΑΜΒΑΝΕΙΣ ΠΡΟΣΩΠΟΝ ("you do not receive face")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "you do not receive [the] face [of anyone]" | Preserves Semitism with brackets |
| LOGOS | "do not receive a face" | Preserves Semitism literally |
| PNEUMA | "show no favoritism" | Renders meaning directly |
| **SOPHIA** | **"you do not show partiality"** | **Independent — clear English preserving the meaning without obscure idiom or excessive informality** |

### 20:25 — ΑΠΟΔΟΤΕ ("give back / render")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "give-back" | Hyphenated |
| LOGOS | "render" | Traditional but archaic |
| PNEUMA | "give back to" | Natural English |
| **SOPHIA** | **"give back"** | **Follows PNEUMA's core — preserves ΑΠΟ- prefix meaning without archaism or hyphens** |

### 20:25 — ΤΟΙΝΥΝ ("then / therefore")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "Therefore" | Heavier particle |
| LOGOS | "Then" | Natural transition |
| PNEUMA | "Then" | Natural transition |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Then"** | **Follows LOGOS and PNEUMA (2-to-1)** |

### 20:35 — ΚΑΤΑΞΙΩΘΕΝΤΕΣ ("counted worthy / considered worthy")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "considered-worthy" | Hyphenated |
| LOGOS | "counted worthy" | Formal, conveys divine reckoning |
| PNEUMA | "considered worthy" | Standard English |
| **SOPHIA** | **"counted worthy"** | **Follows LOGOS — "counted" better conveys deliberate divine reckoning** |

### 20:37 — ΕΜΗΝΥΣΕΝ ("revealed / disclosed")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "disclosed" | Clinical precision |
| LOGOS | "revealed" | Appropriate theological weight |
| PNEUMA | "made this clear" | Paraphrase |
| **SOPHIA** | **"revealed"** | **Follows LOGOS — proper weight for ΜΗΝΥΩ without clinical tone** |

### 20:38 — ΔΕ (transitional particle)
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "But" | Adversative |
| LOGOS | "Now" | Transitional |
| PNEUMA | (omitted) | No rendering |
| **SOPHIA** | **"Now"** | **Follows LOGOS — transitional rather than adversative suits the argumentative flow** |

### 20:45 — ΑΚΟΥΟΝΤΟΣ ΠΑΝΤΟΣ ΤΟΥ ΛΑΟΥ ("in the hearing of all the people")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "[while] all the people [were] hearing" | Brackets required |
| LOGOS | "in the hearing of all the people" | Formal genitive absolute |
| PNEUMA | "while all the people were listening" | Natural paraphrase |
| **SOPHIA** | **"in the hearing of all the people"** | **Follows LOGOS — formal precision without brackets** |

### 20:46 — ΣΤΟΛΑΙΣ ("long robes")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "long-robes" | Hyphenated |
| LOGOS | "long robes" | Standard |
| PNEUMA | "flowing robes" | Interpretive — adds "flowing" |
| **SOPHIA** | **"long robes"** | **Follows LOGOS — standard rendering without hyphen or added adjective** |

### 20:47 — ΠΕΡΙΣΣΟΤΕΡΟΝ ΚΡΙΜΑ ("greater condemnation")
| Translator | Rendering | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| EMET | "more-abundant judgment" | Over-literal, hyphenated |
| LOGOS | "greater condemnation" | Precise comparative |
| PNEUMA | "most severe punishment" | Superlative exceeds comparative; "punishment" interpretive |
| **SOPHIA** | **"greater condemnation"** | **Follows LOGOS — precise comparative; ΚΡΙΜΑ = condemnation, not punishment** |

---

## Summary Statistics

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| Total verses | 47 |
| Full agreement (all three identical or trivially variant) | 25 |
| Disagreements requiring adjudication | 22 |
| SOPHIA follows LOGOS | 14 |
| SOPHIA follows EMET | 1 |
| SOPHIA follows PNEUMA | 2 |
| SOPHIA follows LOGOS + PNEUMA (2-to-1) | 3 |
| SOPHIA independent rendering | 2 |

### Pattern Notes

LOGOS dominated the consensus in this chapter due to its consistent balance between accuracy and readability. EMET's hyphenation and bracketing patterns were the most frequent cause of divergence. PNEUMA's dynamic renderings were occasionally preferred for transparency (20:1 "proclaiming the good news") but more often smoothed too far from the Greek structure. The most significant independent SOPHIA decision was 20:21, where the Semitism ΟΥ ΛΑΜΒΑΝΕΙΣ ΠΡΟΣΩΠΟΝ was rendered "you do not show partiality" — neither preserving the obscure literal idiom (EMET, LOGOS) nor using informal English (PNEUMA).

---

*Compiled by SOPHIA in council with EMET, LOGOS, and PNEUMA.*
